data.title: BlackRock's Robert Mitchnick: Institutional Investors Quietly Accumulating Bitcoin
data.description: BlackRock's Head of Digital Assets, Robert Mitchnick, has revealed that 90% of Bitcoin ETF investors are consistently accumulating Bitcoin, particularly during price dips. According to NS3.AI, Mitchnick noted that institutional investors are also discreetly purchasing Bitcoin. He argued that this ongoing accumulation has not yet been mirrored in Bitcoin's current price.

data.title: Bitcoin Mining's Shift Towards Nuclear Power Increases
data.description: The demand for AI-driven data centers is contributing to a resurgence in U.S. nuclear power. According to NS3.AI, the share of nuclear energy in Bitcoin mining increased from approximately 4% in 2021 to nearly 9% in 2022. Bitcoin miners have been early adopters of colocated nuclear power, exemplified by TeraWulf's 2021 Nautilus Cryptomine project adjacent to the Susquehanna nuclear plant.

data.title: Boeing Faces Delivery Delays Due to 737 Max Wiring Repairs
data.description: Boeing is addressing issues with damaged wiring in up to 25 undelivered 737 Max jets, which is affecting the short-term delivery schedule of these aircraft. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact on Boeing's operations as the company works to resolve the wiring problems. The repairs are necessary to ensure the safety and functionality of the jets before they can be delivered to customers. This situation adds to the challenges Boeing faces in maintaining its delivery timelines and meeting customer expectations. The company is focused on rectifying the issue promptly to minimize disruptions and uphold its commitment to quality and safety standards.

data.title: Iran Arranges Return of Sailors from India Amid Rising Tensions
data.description: Iran has organized transportation to bring approximately 180 Iranian sailors back from India, according to informed sources. Most of these sailors will fly back from the southern Indian city of Kochi. According to Jin10, Iran is also repatriating the bodies of sailors who perished after their vessel was sunk by a U.S. submarine off the coast of Sri Lanka. The incident, which occurred a week ago, has escalated the Middle Eastern conflict, bringing it closer to the Indian region. Sri Lankan officials reported that out of approximately 130 crew members on the Iranian frigate IRIS Dena, around 32 were rescued and hospitalized, while dozens remain missing. Meanwhile, India is negotiating with Iran to ensure the safe passage of its vessels.

data.title: EPA Proposes Easing Restrictions on Carcinogenic Gas Emissions
data.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) has proposed a plan to relax restrictions on the emissions of ethylene oxide, a gas known to be carcinogenic. According to Jin10, this proposal was announced on Friday and has sparked concerns among environmentalists and public health advocates. The EPA's decision comes amid ongoing debates about balancing industrial growth with environmental and health safety. Critics argue that easing these restrictions could lead to increased health risks for communities near industrial facilities. The proposal is now open for public comment, and the EPA will consider feedback before making a final decision.

data.title: Elon Musk Orders Further Layoffs at xAI
data.description: Elon Musk has reportedly instructed xAI to implement additional layoffs, according to Jin10. The decision comes as part of ongoing efforts to streamline operations and reduce costs within the company. This move follows previous rounds of layoffs aimed at optimizing the workforce and enhancing efficiency. The exact number of employees affected by this decision has not been disclosed. xAI, which focuses on artificial intelligence development, continues to face challenges in a competitive tech landscape. The layoffs are expected to impact various departments within the organization as it seeks to realign its strategic priorities.

data.title: STOCKS | Defensive Stocks Fail to Shield Investors
data.description:
Healthcare and consumer staples stocks, traditionally seen as defensive investments, have not provided the expected protection for investors. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that these sectors, typically resilient during economic downturns, have underperformed in recent times. This unexpected trend has left investors questioning the reliability of these stocks as safe havens.

The healthcare sector, often considered a stable investment due to consistent demand, has faced challenges. Factors such as regulatory pressures and changing market dynamics have contributed to its lackluster performance. Similarly, consumer staples, which include essential goods like food and household products, have not delivered the anticipated returns.

Investors have historically turned to these sectors during periods of economic uncertainty, expecting them to provide steady returns. However, the current market environment has disrupted this pattern, leading to a reevaluation of investment strategies.

As the market continues to evolve, investors are urged to reassess their portfolios and consider diversifying their investments to mitigate risks. The traditional view of healthcare and consumer staples as defensive plays may need to be reconsidered in light of recent developments.

data.title: Cumberland-linked wallet withdrew 23,000 ETH, worth $50.1 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, in the past two hours, wallets associated with Cumberland (0xe28...17b5, 0xFC8...B88b) withdrew 23,000 ETH, worth $50.1 million, from Binance and Coinbase.

data.title: Circle's USDC Surpasses Tether's USDT in Transaction Volume Share
data.description: Circle's USDC has captured a 64% share of adjusted transaction volume this year, surpassing Tether's USDT, according to NS3.AI. Despite this shift in transaction volume, USDT remains the leader in supply, with a market capitalization of approximately $184 billion, compared to USDC's roughly $79 billion.

data.title: Chinese Zinc Smelters Experience Supply Disruptions from Iran
data.description: Chinese zinc smelters are encountering challenges due to disruptions in raw material shipments from Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact on supply chains already strained by lower-than-anticipated deliveries from a significant new mine in Russia. This situation adds pressure to the zinc market, affecting production and potentially influencing global zinc prices.

data.title: Circle's USDC Surpasses USDT in Transaction Volume, Mizuho Raises Price Target
data.description: Mizuho has increased its price target for Circle to $120 from $100 following USDC's impressive performance in 2026. According to NS3.AI, USDC has achieved approximately $2.2 trillion in adjusted transaction volume this year, surpassing USDT's $1.3 trillion. This marks a significant shift, as USDC now accounts for about 64% of adjusted volumes, reversing the trend from 2019 to 2025 when USDT consistently led the market.

data.title: Barclays: Expects the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates by 25 basis points each in September 2026 and March 2027.
data.description: Barclays: The Federal Reserve is expected to cut interest rates by 25 basis points each in September 2026 and March 2027, compared to previous forecasts of cuts in June and September of this year. (Golden Ten)

data.title: After a six-month hiatus, a major whale bought 5,003 ETH, worth $10.9 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale (0x2d85) bought 5003 ETH at $2179 each, worth $10.9 million, after a six-month period of inactivity. This address had previously sold ETH at a high of approximately $4300 six months ago.

data.title: Brazilian President Lula Blocks U.S. Official's Visit
data.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva has taken steps to prevent a U.S. official from visiting Brazil. According to Jin10, this decision marks a significant diplomatic move by the Brazilian government. The reasons behind the restriction have not been disclosed, but it reflects ongoing tensions between Brazil and the United States. This development could impact bilateral relations and future diplomatic engagements between the two nations.

data.title: Latin American Nations Call for Halt to Hostile Actions by Iran
data.description: Mexico, Colombia, and Brazil have issued a joint statement urging Iran to cease its hostile actions. According to Jin10, the three Latin American countries expressed their concerns over the escalating tensions involving Iran and called for a peaceful resolution to the ongoing issues. The statement highlights the importance of diplomatic efforts in maintaining regional stability and preventing further conflict. The countries emphasized their commitment to international peace and security, urging all parties involved to engage in constructive dialogue.

data.title: Amazon to Integrate Cerebras Systems Chips with Trainium for Enhanced AI Software Performance
data.description: Amazon has announced its intention to incorporate chips from Cerebras Systems, a startup, alongside its proprietary Trainium processors. Bloomberg posted on X that this strategic move aims to enhance the performance of AI software. The collaboration between Amazon and Cerebras Systems is expected to optimize the processing capabilities required for advanced AI applications. By leveraging the strengths of both chip technologies, Amazon seeks to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of its AI operations.

data.title: Vivid Seats Faces Financial Struggles Amid Earnings Miss
data.description: Vivid Seats's debt has fallen deeper into distressed levels following the company's earnings report, which failed to meet expectations. Bloomberg posted on X that the ticket reseller also projected weaker financial performance for the remainder of the year. The disappointing earnings and guidance have raised concerns about the company's financial stability.

data.title: Canada's Economy Faces Major Adjustments Due to U.S. Trade Actions
data.description: Canadian Prime Minister Carney has highlighted significant economic adjustments in Canada as a result of recent U.S. trade measures. According to Jin10, these trade actions have prompted shifts in Canada's economic landscape, necessitating strategic responses from policymakers. The adjustments underscore the interconnected nature of the North American economies and the impact of international trade policies on domestic economic conditions.

data.title: India to Cancel IDBI Bank Stake Sale Due to Low Bids
data.description: India plans to cancel the bids received for a majority stake in IDBI Bank as they fell short of the minimum price required, according to sources. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the decision comes after evaluating the financial offers submitted by potential buyers. The government had aimed to divest its stake in the bank to raise funds and reduce its involvement in the banking sector. However, the bids did not meet the financial expectations set by the authorities, leading to the decision to scrap the sale process. The move reflects the challenges faced in attracting investors willing to meet the valuation criteria for state-owned assets. The government will now reassess its strategy for the bank's divestment, considering alternative approaches to achieve its financial objectives.

data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Launches 'Real Commitment-4' Operation
data.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard announced the commencement of the 45th wave of the 'Real Commitment-4' operation on Friday evening. According to Jin10, this action follows a significant response from the Iranian people to the Supreme Leader and their participation in the International Quds Day march. The operation underscores the ongoing political and social dynamics within the country, reflecting the government's engagement with its citizens and international events.
